query: Orchestrate the repo: keep 8 agents going and react to merges as they land.
note: Explicit orchestrate + keep N agents going + event reaction = the slot loop.

query: Fill your slots and work the queue P0-first.
note: "Fill your slots" / "work the queue" are named trigger phrases.

query: Run a few rounds over the backlog and keep the merge pipeline moving.
note: Running rounds / keeping the pipeline moving is the orchestration loop.

query: fix
note: Bare "fix" with no specific issue is the explicit bare-fix trigger — pull next N and dispatch.

query: Keep 4 agents busy on issues and refill a slot whenever one merges or fails.
note: Slot accounting + event-driven refill across many PRs is the loop, not a single dispatch.

query: Babysit the merge queue for the next couple hours and dispatch as slots free up.
note: Monitor/babysit the queue + slot-driven dispatch = orchestration.

query: Is the queue genuinely backed up right now, or is that just normal CI churn?
note: Backup detection (the three-condition rule) is an orchestration decision.

query: Should I dispatch another agent, or am I already saturated and should hold?
note: Deciding whether to fill another slot / apply backpressure is the loop's job.

query: Start working through the open issues, a handful at a time, and keep it going.
note: Casual; continuous batched throughput over the backlog is the N-slot loop.

query: Pause all dispatch across every session, then resume in an hour.
note: The pause/resume sentinel-issue mechanism is orchestration state.

query: Spawn one agent to implement #214 and write its dispatch brief.
note: A single dispatch with its brief is dispatching-subagents, not the slot loop.

query: PR #190 is stuck BLOCKED and green — why won't it merge?
note: One PR's stuck-state diagnosis is driving-prs-to-merge.

query: CI went red on #205 — read the failing job log and fix it.
note: Single-PR CI triage belongs to driving-prs-to-merge despite queue overlap.

query: An agent stalled and returned status:failed — salvage its worktree or re-dispatch?
note: Stalled-agent salvage is a dispatch concern, not the orchestration loop.

query: Define the agent-model and agent-effort labels and bootstrap them in this repo.
note: The control-field taxonomy/bootstrap is gh-issue-labels; the loop only reads these.

query: Claim #88 before anyone else and drop the lock comment.
note: The lock/claim protocol is gh-issue-locking; orchestration calls it but doesn't define it.

query: We've got a backlog of vague feature requests — let's groom them with the PM until they're dev-ready.
note: Grooming raw issues to intent is grooming-issues; 'backlog' is a lure, no dispatch loop.

query: Recon these five issues in parallel and tell me which are ready to dispatch.
note: Parallel read-only recon to size issues is technical-recon, not slot-filling with implementers.

query: Turn this bug report into a properly groomed, labeled GitHub issue.
note: Filing one trackable issue is gh-issue-filing, upstream of any orchestration.

query: Our restaurant's reservation system is overbooking Friday nights — can you look into the slot logic?
note: Truly unrelated: 'slots' here are dinner reservations, not agent concurrency slots.
